name: smith hack squat
instructions: Adjust the barbell on the smith machine to an appropriate height for your body.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly pointed outwards.
Position yourself under the barbell, resting it on your upper traps and shoulders.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and keep your chest up as you unrack the barbell.
Take a step back and position your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees and lower your body down, keeping your chest up and back straight.
Continue descending until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Adjust the barbell on the smith machine to an appropriate height for your body. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly pointed outwards. Position yourself under the barbell, resting it on your upper traps and shoulders. Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Engage your core and keep your chest up as you unrack the barbell. Take a step back and position your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Bend your knees and lower your body down, keeping your chest up and back straight. Continue descending until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below. Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
